blurb: DeepMind's recent output straddles UK government partnership and frontier-safety research: an AI planning-decisions prototype built with UK government, plus an 'AI Control Roadmap' for securing AI agents and a $10M multi-agent-safety funding call.

publications.title: Unlocking UK house-building with AI-accelerated planning
publications.summary: DeepMind and the UK government announce a prototype aimed at faster housing/planning decisions. London-relevant: this is a frontier lab embedding directly into a UK government delivery priority (planning reform), the kind of public-sector partnership that shapes a lab's domestic policy standing.

publications.title: Securing the future of AI agents
publications.summary: Introduces an 'AI Control Roadmap' combining traditional safeguards with real-time monitoring to secure internal agent systems. Agent security is becoming its own policy sub-domain; worth watching whether this language shows up in DeepMind's regulatory engagement.

publications.title: Investing in multi-agent AI safety research
publications.summary: DeepMind and partners announce a $10M funding call for multi-agent safety research. Funding-the-field move; signals where the lab thinks the next safety frontier sits.

notes: blogRssUrl https://deepmind.google/blog/rss.xml confirmed working (note its <atom:link self> is a placeholder example.com URL, but item links are valid).
